# Investigative Narrative — Case SRL18-DC-DISK

*Generated by Council-SIFT from its verification substrate (`claw-memory-core`). Every claim below is
**COUNCIL_VERIFIED**: it survived adversarial review by the forensic seats, the evidence it cites resolves
in real tool output, and it is traceable to the exact tool execution; concrete-command receipts may be rerun with `csift trace --rerun`, while stored-output rehash remains the baseline integrity check.*

## Summary

4 of 8 drafted finding(s) are Council-verified; 4 draft(s) were
refuted by the Council and self-corrected **before reaching this report** (no human intervention). The
verified picture:

## Findings

### F-analyst-SRL18-DC-DISK-001 — fls -r -l of inode 69647 (C:\temp) on the DC NTFS volume lists … *(confidence: HIGH)*

fls -r -l of inode 69647 (C:\temp) on the DC NTFS volume lists two subdirectories created 2018-09-05: 'Active Directory' containing 'ntds.dit' (67,108,864 bytes) and 'ntds.jfm' (16,384 bytes), and 'registry' containing 'SYSTEM' (16,252,928 bytes) and 'SECURITY' (65,536 bytes).. The co-located set ntds.dit + ntds.jfm (the Active Directory database and its ESE checkpoint) together with the SYSTEM and SECURITY registry hives, staged under C:\temp\Active Directory and C:\temp\registry, is the canonical on-disk output of an 'ntdsutil ... ifm create full c:\temp' (Install-From-Media) operation. This is a domain-controller credential-theft artifact: ntds.dit holds every domain account's password hash and the SYSTEM hive supplies the boot key needed to decrypt them. The folders are dated 2018-09-05, well after DC provisioning..

- **Evidence pointer:** `C:\temp (NTFS root) — Active Directory\ntds.dit, registry\SYSTEM, registry\SECURITY#inode 69647 (C:\temp); ntds.dit inode 132832; SYSTEM inode 132833; SECURITY inode 132834; created 2018-09-05 12:16-12:17 UTC` — tool `fls (The Sleuth Kit)`, command `fls -r -l "/mnt/evidence/Compromised APT Attack Scenarios/SRL 2018/base-dc-cdrive.E01" 69…`
- **Cited evidence:** `ntds.dit`, `ntds.jfm`, `SYSTEM`, `SECURITY`, `Active Directory`, `registry`, `2018-09-05 12:17:02 (UTC)`, `67108864`, `16252928` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 304fde7d184b027b…` — stored-output re-hash verified by `csift trace F-analyst-SRL18-DC-DISK-001`; `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-SRL18-DC-DISK-001` is the stronger independent check when the recorded command is concrete and the evidence path is present.
- **Council Receipt:** `55953c3e72b4fad8…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED

### F-analyst-SRL18-DC-DISK-004 — icat of inode 131829 — a ConsoleHost_history.txt file located w… *(confidence: MEDIUM)*

icat of inode 131829 — a ConsoleHost_history.txt file located within the C:\Users\rsydow-a profile (AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\PSReadline) — recovers 291 lines of PowerShell command history. It contains: numerous 'Enter-PSSession' connections to named hosts (e.g. base-admin, base-hunt); loops over host-inventory files (srl-all.txt, srl-workstations.txt) running Invoke-Command against every host; 17 'wmic /node:<ip> shadowcopy call create volume=C:\' commands targeting the workstation/server fleet; a 'Rename-Item ...Google\Update-DISABLED ...Google\Update' plus 'Start-Service gupdate'/'gupdatem' and execution of GoogleUpdate.exe pushed to all workstations; fleet-wide 'VMwareToolboxCmd.exe ... timesync disable'; repeated 'get-winevent -LogName Security -oldest -maxevents 1'; and 'find-module -name pagefilemodder*'.. The recorded commands constitute fleet-wide remote administration originating FROM this domain controller, using PowerShell remoting (Enter-PSSession/Invoke-Command) and WMIC against the full host inventory. The specific techniques the history records are: bulk Volume Shadow Copy creation across the host fleet (a known method for reading locked system files such as registry hives and ntds.dit), disabling of VMware guest time synchronization across hosts, re-enabling and starting a previously-DISABLED 'GoogleUpdate' service/binary on workstations, per-host enumeration of the oldest Security event, and a search for a 'pagefilemodder' module. These are the literal operations the file records. This artifact does not establish which principal ran them or for what purpose; it should be correlated with the local C:\temp ntds.dit dump (finding-001) and with authentication/event-log evidence before any conclusion about actor or intent.. This is the **corrected** finding — an initial, stronger reading was refuted by the Council (see *Self-correction*, below) and the analyst re-filed only what the evidence supports.

- **Evidence pointer:** `C:\Users\rsydow-a\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\PSReadline\ConsoleHost_history.txt#inode 131829` — tool `icat (The Sleuth Kit)`, command `icat "/mnt/evidence/Compromised APT Attack Scenarios/SRL 2018/base-dc-cdrive.E01" 131829`
- **Cited evidence:** `wmic /node:172.16.7.11 shadowcopy call create volume=C:\`, `Rename-Item 'C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\Update-DISABLED' 'C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\Update'`, `timesync disable`, `find-module -name pagefilemodder*`, `Enter-PSSession base-admin`, `get-winevent -LogName Security -oldest -maxevents 1`, `Start-Service gupdate` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 34f1d9865620e9e9…` — stored-output re-hash verified by `csift trace F-analyst-SRL18-DC-DISK-004`; `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-SRL18-DC-DISK-004` is the stronger independent check when the recorded command is concrete and the evidence path is present.
- **Council Receipt:** `4529a74a3cee8fd1…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED

### F-analyst-SRL18-DC-DISK-007 — Parsing Security.evtx (icat inode 103887 -> /tmp/sec.evtx) and … *(confidence: MEDIUM)*

Parsing Security.evtx (icat inode 103887 -> /tmp/sec.evtx) and extracting all 4697 'service installed' records (event identifier 0x00001259) yields 12 events, all on computer base-dc.shieldbase.lan, dated Sep 06 2018. Two are non-standard: a service named 'F-Response Subject' with image 'C:\windows\subject_srv.exe -s "base-hunt.shieldbase.lan:5682" -l 3262 -v "F-Response Subject" -k "155522845"', and a service named 'mnemosyne' with image 'C:\windows/Mnemosyne.sys' (this driver service installed three times). The remaining ten are routine Windows Defender driver services (MpKsl657a2bb2, MpKsl68a89100) and per-user svchost services ('svchost.exe -k UnistackSvcGroup').. Two of the installed services on this DC are not native OS components: (a) 'F-Response Subject' / subject_srv.exe is consistent with a commercial remote disk-and-memory forensic-acquisition agent, configured by its command line to connect to host base-hunt.shieldbase.lan on TCP 5682; (b) 'mnemosyne' is a kernel-mode driver (.sys) loaded from C:\windows, recorded as installed three times. The remaining ten are routine Windows Defender driver services (MpKsl*) and per-user svchost services. These Security-log 4697 records indicate that service installations for a remote-acquisition-style agent and a kernel driver were logged on this DC on Sep 06 2018. This may be material context for the shadow-copy/collection commands in finding-004 and the staged C:\temp copies in finding-001; the log alone does not indicate the authorization or purpose of those activities.. This is the **corrected** finding — an initial, stronger reading was refuted by the Council (see *Self-correction*, below) and the analyst re-filed only what the evidence supports.

- **Evidence pointer:** `C:\Windows\System32\winevt\Logs\Security.evtx (Microsoft-Windows-Security-Auditing, EID 4697)#inode 103887; 4697 records at Sep 06 2018 (e.g. 08:30:02 UTC); host base-dc.shieldbase.lan` — tool `evtxexport (libevtx) on icat-extracted Security.evtx`, command `icat <img> 103887 > /tmp/sec.evtx ; evtxexport -f text /tmp/sec.evtx ; awk 'BEGIN{RS="\n\…`
- **Cited evidence:** `0x00001259 (4697)`, `F-Response Subject`, `subject_srv.exe -s "base-hunt.shieldbase.lan:5682"`, `mnemosyne`, `C:\windows/Mnemosyne.sys`, `base-dc.shieldbase.lan`, `MpKsl657a2bb2`, `svchost.exe -k UnistackSvcGroup` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 149adb056be25ef5…` — stored-output re-hash verified by `csift trace F-analyst-SRL18-DC-DISK-007`; `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-SRL18-DC-DISK-007` is the stronger independent check when the recorded command is concrete and the evidence path is present.
- **Council Receipt:** `e98bfb60e0fb75a2…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED

### F-analyst-SRL18-DC-DISK-008 — Counting account-management event IDs in the parsed Security.ev… *(confidence: MEDIUM)*

Counting account-management event IDs in the parsed Security.evtx (base-dc) gives: 4720 account-created=0, 4722 enabled=0, 4726 deleted=0, 4728 added-to-global-group=0, 4732 added-to-local-group=0, 4756 added-to-universal-group=0, 4741 computer-account-created=0, 4724 password-reset=1, 4738 account-changed=1. The single 4724 record (0x00001274) and single 4738 record (0x00001282) both target the domain account tyler.oslund and are both timestamped Sep 06 2018 01:37:23 UTC on base-dc.shieldbase.lan. In the 4724 record the subject-name string is rsydow-a; in the 4738 record the subject-name string is ANONYMOUS LOGON.. Within this Security log's retained window there is no record of any new domain account being created (4720=0) and no record of any security-group membership being added (4728/4732/4756=0), nor any computer account creation (4741=0). The only account-management actions recorded are one password reset (4724) and one account modification (4738), both against the pre-existing account tyler.oslund at the same instant (2018-09-06 01:37:23 UTC). The 4724 record's subject fields name rsydow-a as the account that performed the reset. A domain password reset is a privileged operation; this record does not indicate whether it was authorized. The practical bearing on the 'unauthorized accounts' lead: the retained Security log holds no evidence of new or newly-group-privileged accounts being created during its coverage, so the on-disk profiles for other accounts were not accompanied by account-creation events inside this window.. This is the **corrected** finding — an initial, stronger reading was refuted by the Council (see *Self-correction*, below) and the analyst re-filed only what the evidence supports.

- **Evidence pointer:** `C:\Windows\System32\winevt\Logs\Security.evtx (account-management EIDs 4720/4724/4738/4728/4732/4756/4741)#inode 103887; 4724+4738 records at 2018-09-06 01:37:23 UTC; target tyler.oslund (SID ...-1180)` — tool `grep/awk over evtxexport text of Security.evtx`, command `evtxexport -f text /tmp/sec.evtx ; grep -c per account-mgmt EID + awk RS extract of 0x127…`
- **Cited evidence:** `4720_account_created=0`, `4728_added_to_global_group=0`, `4732_added_to_local_group=0`, `4756_added_to_universal_group=0`, `4724_password_reset=1`, `4738_account_changed=1`, `0x00001274 (4724)`, `tyler.oslund`, `rsydow-a`, `base-dc.shieldbase.lan` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 75d7743e4fb98635…` — stored-output re-hash verified by `csift trace F-analyst-SRL18-DC-DISK-008`; `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-SRL18-DC-DISK-008` is the stronger independent check when the recorded command is concrete and the evidence path is present.
- **Council Receipt:** `db7e7ba3de487651…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED

## What the evidence does NOT support (Council-enforced discipline)

The Council refused the following stronger readings because the available artifacts do not establish them.
They are recorded here explicitly so the report distinguishes **confirmed** from **inferred or unfounded**:

- Inference overreach (ATTRIBUTION): attribution to a specific actor cannot be established from one artifact — corroborate with intel + multiple artifacts. Offending clause: "The mass creation of Volume Shadow Copies across every host, fleet-wide disabling of guest time synchronization, re-enabling and starting a ".
- Inference overreach (ATTRIBUTION): attribution to a specific actor cannot be established from one artifact — corroborate with intel + multiple artifacts. Offending clause: "These actions are dual-use, consistent with BOTH authorized incident-response/threat-hunting collection (note 'hunt' hostnames) AND adversar".
- Cited token(s) NOT present in the tool output as a standalone token (hallucinated/unsupported): MpKsl.
- Inference overreach (ATTRIBUTION): attribution to a specific actor cannot be established from one artifact — corroborate with intel + multiple artifacts. Offending clause: "dit/registry copies (finding-001) are, on disk alone, indistinguishable between authorized forensic collection and adversary credential thef".
- Inference overreach (CERTAINTY): unjustified certainty — a single artifact rarely proves a conclusion outright. Offending clause: "This record establishes that remote forensic-acquisition and kernel-driver service installations occurred on this domain controller on Sep 0".

## Confidence & limitations

- Confidence is stated per finding (HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / SPECULATIVE) and reflects what a single artifact
  can bear; nothing here is asserted beyond the cited evidence.
- "Observation" is the literal tool output; "interpretation" is the analyst's inference. The Council's
  Citation, Tool-semantics, Contradiction, Inference, and Scope seats independently checked the leap between them; Synthesis aggregated the verdicts.
- Every finding traces to its tool execution (`csift trace F-analyst-SRL18-DC-DISK-001`); concrete-command receipts can additionally be re-executed against evidence with `csift trace --rerun …`. Council Receipts are hash-chained.
- This narrative is generated from the audit substrate, not hand-written — it cannot contain a claim that
  is not a verified node in the graph.
